SDK_ABSTRACTION_REQUIRED.description: SDK must abstract LiveKit into a simple consumer API
rules: Consumers must not interact with LiveKit directly
SDK must expose unified callbacks
SDK must handle initialization internally
SDK must unify connection, signaling, and lifecycle

ARCH_VOICE_CALL_SDK_FACADE_V1.description: VoiceCallSdk.liveKit is the primary facade for sdk_connect voice calls and owns CallEngine plus LiveKit media lifecycle
rules: VoiceCallSdk.liveKit manages CallEngine creation and disposal
VoiceCallSdk.liveKit manages LiveKit media engine lifecycle internally
Example app and README should use VoiceCallSdk as the primary integration path

ARCH_CALL_ENGINE_SSOT_V2.description: CallEngine remains the single source of truth even when wrapped by VoiceCallSdk
rules: Call state mutations flow through CallEngine
Facade layers must not duplicate call lifecycle state
Unified callbacks may observe state but not replace engine authority

SEC_SIGNAL_VALIDATION_REQUIRED_V1.description: Signal validation is mandatory before VoiceCallSdk processes call setup
rules: VoiceCallSdk must reject invalid or incomplete signaling payloads
Token and signaling validation remain required preconditions for call setup

SEC_SECURE_RANDOM_CALL_ID_V1.description: Call identifiers must use secure random generation rather than timestamps
rules: Timestamp-based call IDs are not permitted
Generated call IDs must be suitable for external signaling contexts

TEST_WIDGET_SKIP_USER_APPROVED_V1.description: A known flaky widget test may remain skipped when explicitly approved by the user and the rest of the suite passes
rules: Document the intentional skip in memory or task notes
Keep the skip scoped to the flaky test only
Preserve passing coverage for the remaining suite

ARCH_SDKCONNECT_PUBLIC_FACADE_V1.description: SDKConnect is the neutral public facade and documented entry point for sdk_connect
rules: SDKConnect is the primary and documented public entry point
Barrel exports are narrowed to SDKConnect facade and core models
Callbacks and events are unified under SDKConnect
Voice flow uses callType=voice and reserves video for additive expansion
Example app and README use SDKConnect.create as the integration path
VoiceCallSdk remains an internal compatibility layer behind SDKConnect

ARCH_VIDEO_CALL_ADDITIVE_V1.description: Video support added additively via SDKConnectCallType.video; no breaking changes to voice path
rules: startCall/acceptCall auto-enable camera when callType=video via CallEngine.setVideoEnabled
VoiceCallSdk.setVideoEnabled and toggleCamera are wired through to CallEngine
Voice callType remains unaffected by video additions

ARCH_SDKCONNECT_VOICE_VIDEO_FACADE_SPLIT.description: SDKConnect exposes a neutral public facade while voice/video concerns remain split behind facade layers with CallEngine as SSOT
rules: SDKConnect owns the public voice/video entry surface
Facade split must not duplicate lifecycle authority already owned by CallEngine
Voice and video wrappers propagate callType into CallEngine rather than inferring it later

RULE_CALLTYPE_PROPAGATION_REQUIRED_V1.description: Call type must propagate from facade entrypoints into CallEngine lifecycle decisions
rules: startCall and acceptCall must carry explicit callType through the facade boundary
CallEngine state and downstream media decisions derive from propagated callType
Defaulting or re-deriving callType after facade entry is not permitted when the caller already chose it

MEDIA_VIDEO_CAMERA_V1.description: MediaEngine extended with camera control; CallState tracks video enablement
rules: MediaEngine interface exposes setCameraOn(bool) and isVideoEnabled
LiveKitMediaEngine implements setCameraOn via localParticipant.setCameraEnabled
CallState includes isVideoEnabled field as source of truth for video state

SEC_TOKEN_EXPIRY_CHECK_V1.description: JWT expiry enforced in CallEngine before media connect
rules: CallEngine._validateConnectParams decodes JWT payload via base64url
exp claim is checked against current clock time
Expired tokens throw CallLifecycleException before any media connection attempt

SEC_SIGNAL_REJECT_EMITS_ERROR_V1.description: Invalid or rejected signals emit VoiceCallErrorEvent instead of silent drop
rules: VoiceCallSdk._handleSignal emits VoiceCallErrorEvent on invalid envelope (signal.validation_failed)
signalValidator rejection emits VoiceCallErrorEvent (signal.validation_rejected)
Silent signal drops are not permitted

SEC_ACCEPT_CALLTYPE_MISMATCH_GUARD_V1.description: Accept flows must reject callType mismatches before joining media or mutating active call state
rules: acceptCall validates that requested callType matches the pending/incoming session type
Mismatched accept attempts fail fast with a guard error
Media join and state transition must not proceed after a callType mismatch
New tests cover: video start enables camera, setVideoEnabled toggle, expired JWT rejection, malformed JWT, signalValidator rejection emits error, invalid envelope emits error
Widget test skip remains intentional per TEST_WIDGET_SKIP_USER_APPROVED_V1

TEST_CALLTYPE_GUARDS_V1.description: Facade split and accept mismatch guards are covered by targeted tests
rules: Tests verify callType propagates through SDKConnect facade into CallEngine
Tests verify acceptCall rejects mismatched callType before connection side effects

ARCH_PRODUCTION_RELIABILITY_SDKCONNECT_V.description: SDKConnect reliability layer supports resilient real-time calling under fluctuating network conditions
rules: CallEngine supports automatic reconnect orchestration after transient disconnects
ICE recovery hook is available to trigger transport/path recovery workflows
Silent token refresh hook can renew auth without forcing user-visible teardown
Adaptive audio-priority fallback can degrade media strategy to preserve call continuity
Network quality thresholds are configurable and not hard-coded
Reconnect, recovered, and token lifecycle events are surfaced to consumers

SEC_SILENT_TOKEN_REFRESH_HOOK_V1.description: Token rotation path is designed for uninterrupted sessions
rules: Token refresh hooks must avoid exposing raw token values in logs/events
Refresh failures emit explicit lifecycle events for caller-controlled handling
Recovered auth state must revalidate connect preconditions before media escalation

ARCH_LIFECYCLE_SAFETY_V1.description: Production-grade lifecycle safety: strict phase transitions, serialized operations, app lifecycle and interruption handling
rules: Invalid phase transitions (e.g., connecting→connecting) are rejected by CallEngine guard
All state-mutating operations are enqueued and executed serially to prevent races
App lifecycle events (foreground/background/interrupted) are handled internally by CallEngine
Interruption recovery (calls/notifications/audio-session loss) restores state without consumer action
Audio route change events are surfaced via callback; speaker/routing decisions remain consumer-controlled

ARCH_SERIALIZED_MEDIA_EVENTS_V1.description: Media-event mutations are serialized through the CallEngine internal queue
rules: All media state changes (mute, camera, speaker) are dispatched through the engine queue
Signal replay cache is bounded to prevent unbounded memory growth
Signal replay cache is cleared when CallEngine returns to idle phase

SEC_RECONNECT_TOKEN_RACE_GUARD_V1.description: Reconnect and token refresh paths are guarded against concurrent execution races
rules: A reconnect in-progress flag prevents parallel reconnect attempts
Token refresh guards prevent a stale token from racing a fresh one into media connect
Race guard state is reset only after the guarded operation fully resolves or fails

ARCH_CALL_ENGINE_SERIALIZED_OPS.description: All CallEngine state-mutating operations are serialized through a single _operationQueue chain
rules: start, accept, reject, end, mute, speaker, video, and lifecycle ops all chain through _operationQueue
Media event handlers route through the queue via _runSerialized
No state-mutating path bypasses the queue

ARCH_LIFECYCLE_OBSERVER_V1.description: VoiceCallSdk registers as WidgetsBindingObserver internally with safety guards
rules: Register/unregister wrapped in try/catch to be safe in non-widget test environments
Observer registration is managed by VoiceCallSdk, not consumer code

ARCH_INTERRUPTION_RECOVERY_V1.description: CallEngine tracks _isInterrupted flag and recovers media session after interruptions
rules: paused/inactive/hidden lifecycle states emit interruptionStarted
resumed after interruption triggers _restoreMediaSession
_restoreMediaSession reconnects if needed and restores mute/speaker/video state

ARCH_AUDIO_ROUTE_EVENTS_V1.description: setSpeakerOn emits audioRouteChanged event from CallEngine
rules: CallEngine emits audioRouteChanged after setSpeakerOn
LiveKitMediaEngine emits audioRouteChanged MediaEngineEvent on setSpeakerOn

ARCH_TOKEN_REFRESH_DEDUP_V1.description: Token refresh deduplication via _tokenRefreshInFlight shared future
rules: Concurrent reconnect paths share the same _tokenRefreshInFlight future
tokenRefresher is not called multiple times during concurrent reconnects
Guard is set before reconnect logic begins and cleared on completion or failure

ARCH_STRICT_TRANSITIONS_V1.description: _isTransitionAllowed matrix enforces valid FSM edges at every _transition call
rules: Invalid phase transitions are rejected before any side effects occur
All state-mutation paths invoke _transition through the guarded matrix

SEC_REPLAY_BOUNDED_CACHE_V1.description: Signal replay dedup set is bounded to 512 entries with LRU eviction
rules: LRU order queue tracks eviction order; oldest entry removed when limit reached
Cache is cleared on idle transition to prevent stale replay entries

ARCH_ACTION_DEBOUNCE_V1.description: _isActionDebounced with per-action key and 350ms default window prevents burst duplicates
rules: Each action uses its own key in the debounce map
Default debounce window is 350ms unless overridden per action

ARCH_EXAMPLE_VOICE_VIDEO_STRUCTURE_V1.description: Example app usage is organized by feature folders with centralized bootstrap and config
rules: example/main.dart remains the bootstrap entry point
SDK configuration is centralized in example/config/config_sdk.dart
Incoming call UI is isolated in example/call_screen/incoming_call_screen.dart
Voice and video call screens live in example/voice/voice_call_screen.dart and example/video/video_call_screen.dart

ARCH_MEDIA_SESSION_ONLY_OWNERSHIP_V1.description: SDKConnect architecture enforces media/session ownership in SDK facade and CallEngine while removing signaling ownership from SDK and engine layers
rules: SDK and CallEngine do not own signaling transport or signaling orchestration
CallEngine remains the SSOT for call lifecycle and state transitions
Lifecycle phases are strictly limited to idle, connecting, connected, reconnecting, disconnected, failed
Media/session orchestration remains internal while signaling integration is externalized

ARCH_CALL_ENGINE_SSOT_V3.description: CallEngine is the authoritative single source of truth for runtime call state
rules: No facade, SDK wrapper, or signaling adapter duplicates lifecycle ownership
All lifecycle transitions and state mutations are derived from CallEngine
Public callbacks and events reflect engine state instead of creating parallel state

ARCH_LIFECYCLE_PHASE_SET_V1.description: Allowed lifecycle phases are fixed and enforced for interoperability and safety
rules: Allowed phases: idle, connecting, connected, reconnecting, disconnected, failed
No additional implicit phases are introduced by wrappers or adapters
Transition guards must reject unknown or out-of-contract phase changes

ARCH_RECONNECT_REFRESH_DEDUP_V2.description: Reconnect and token refresh paths are deduplicated to avoid race conditions
rules: Concurrent reconnect attempts are coalesced into a single in-flight operation
Concurrent token refresh requests share one in-flight future/promise
Dedup guards are reset only after operation completion or terminal failure

SEC_PUBLIC_ERROR_SANITIZATION_V1.description: Errors emitted through public callbacks/events are sanitized before exposure
rules: No raw internal exceptions or sensitive payload fragments are exposed publicly
Public error events use stable, non-sensitive codes/messages
Sanitization applies to callback, stream, and event-based public surfaces

RULE_P2P_MAX_2_ENFORCED_V1.description: Peer-to-peer sessions are limited to two participants
rules: P2P call model must enforce a maximum of 2 participants
Scaling beyond 2 requires a different topology contract (e.g., SFU)

ARCH_CONNECTION_LOSS_SINGLE_SOURCE_V1.description: Connection-loss signaling is centralized and emitted from a single terminal lifecycle source
rules: onConnectionLost must not be emitted from multiple reconnect or participant-leave paths
Terminal disconnect callbacks must be deduplicated
Connection-loss emission must remain lifecycle-state driven

ARCH_WIDGET_RENDER_PURITY_V1.description: SDKConnect widgets must remain render-only and avoid realtime lifecycle side effects in build paths
rules: Widget build methods must not trigger lifecycle side effects
Call-ended callbacks must be handled through listeners/subscriptions
Realtime lifecycle orchestration must remain outside widget rendering

ARCH_REMOTE_PARTICIPANT_TERMINATION_V1.description: Remote participant leave is treated as terminal session termination instead of reconnect scenario
rules: Remote participant leave must immediately terminate active session
Participant leave must not trigger reconnect flow
Session teardown must remain deterministic after remote leave

ARCH_TERMINAL_EVENT_DEDUP_V1.description: Terminal lifecycle callbacks are protected against duplicate emission
rules: Disconnected/ended terminal callbacks must emit once per lifecycle termination
Reconnect-failed and participant-left paths must not duplicate terminal callbacks
Lifecycle terminal emission uses fingerprint/guard protection
